input.command:
gh pr edit 8511 --title "spike(pnpm): isolated node-linker — build+tests green via webpack; Turbopack blocked upstream" --body "> **Draft / spike.** Now demonstrates a **fully working configuration** under the isolated linker. Revisit after #8498–#8505 land.

## Status: build + tests green under \`nodeLinker: isolated\` ✅

- \`pnpm build\` → **12/12 tasks, exit 0** (web compiles in ~4.6 min via webpack, full route manifest + middleware)
- \`pnpm test\` → **21/21 tasks, exit 0** (web: 503 files / 6230 tests)
- \`pnpm lint\` → fails only in \`packages/types\` under the old type-aware ESLint 8 setup (this branch predates #8505; the flat-config migration resolves it)

## The Turbopack panic — root-caused, three fixes ruled out

\`next build\` (Turbopack) panics under isolated: \`internal error: entered unreachable code: there must be a path to a root\` (\`module_graph/mod.rs:662\`, inside \`PlainIssue::from_issue → ImportTracer::get_traces\` — i.e. while rendering an issue's import trace). Systematically tried:

| Attempt | Result |
|---|---|
| \`turbopack.root\` pinned to monorepo root | ❌ panic persists |
| Next 16.2.6 → **16.2.10** | ❌ panic persists |
| Externalize \`typeorm\`/\`@boxyhq/saml-jackson\` (source of the 8 unresolvable-optional-driver issues) | ❌ panic persists |

Upstream ([vercel/next.js#82277](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/82277)) attributes this panic to **circular imports**; \`madge\` confirms real runtime cycles in the app (e.g. \`lib/organization/service ↔ lib/user/service\`, an \`email → lingodotdev → auth → sso\` chain). Crucially the same cycles + same Next version build fine under \`hoisted\` — the isolated graph shape (peer-suffixed \`.pnpm\` module instances) is the differentiator. **Verdict: upstream Turbopack bug**, worth filing with our repro.

## The working path: webpack

\`next build --webpack\` compiles cleanly (warnings only). This branch flips the web \`build\` script to \`--webpack\` as the spike tradeoff. Note: \`next dev\` still runs Turbopack and is likely affected — dev under isolated would need \`--webpack\` too, or the upstream fix.

## Second commit also surfaced more phantom deps (now declared)

\`playwright\`/\`@playwright/test\` (web typecheck), \`dotenv-cli\` (web+surveys test scripts used database's hoisted binary), \`@vitejs/plugin-react\` (web vitest config), \`zod\` (surveys test), and the subtlest one: **\`pino\` in \`packages/database\`** — its vite build couldn't resolve pino (undeclared) so it silently **inlined** it, which broke pino's worker-thread spawn at runtime (\`dist/lib/worker.js\` not found). Declaring pino restores externalization. A nice demonstration of how the hoisted layout hides real bugs.

## Options from here

1. **Merge-ready path (recommended):** extract the dependency declarations to \`hoisted\`-based PRs (ENG-1681/ENG-1693 + sweep) now; keep this branch as the isolated proof; file the Turbopack repro upstream; flip when fixed (or accept webpack builds).
2. **Ship isolated + webpack now:** works today (this branch), at the cost of slower builds and a dev-server story to solve.
3. Optionally: break the app's real import cycles regardless — they're worth fixing on their own.

Spike for ENG-1674

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)" 2>&1 | tail -1

content.text:
Done — and the extra iteration paid off. Draft PR [#8511](https://github.com/formbricks/formbricks/pull/8511) is now a **working isolated-linker configuration**, not a blocked one:

## What changed since "blocked"

**Build + tests are green under `nodeLinker: isolated`:**
- `pnpm build` → 12/12 tasks, exit 0 (web app compiles fully — route manifest, middleware, the lot)
- `pnpm test` → 21/21 tasks, exit 0

## How the Turbopack panic was run down

I tried three targeted fixes, each ruled out empirically: `turbopack.root` pinned to the monorepo root ❌, Next 16.2.6 → 16.2.10 ❌, externalizing `typeorm`/`saml-jackson` (source of the 8 unresolvable-optional-driver warnings the panicking import-tracer was rendering) ❌. Then found the upstream thread — [vercel/next.js#82277](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/82277) attributes this exact panic to **circular imports**, and `madge` confirmed real cycles in the app (`lib/organization/service ↔ lib/user/service`, an `email → lingodotdev → auth → sso` chain). Same cycles + same Next version build fine under `hoisted`, so it's the isolated graph shape that trips Turbopack: a genuine **upstream bug**, now with a clean repro worth filing.

**The unblock:** `next build --webpack` compiles cleanly — the spike flips the web build script to webpack. (Caveat flagged in the PR: `next dev` also defaults to Turbopack, so dev would need `--webpack` too until upstream fixes it.)

## Bonus finds along the way

Getting tests green surfaced five more phantom deps, including the best bug of the night: `packages/database`'s vite build couldn't resolve undeclared `pino`, so it silently **inlined** it — breaking pino's worker-thread spawn at runtime. Declaring the dep restored externalization. That one was invisible under hoisting and is exactly the class of latent bug this migration exists to eliminate. Also declared: `playwright`, `dotenv-cli` (web + surveys were borrowing database's hoisted binary), `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `zod`.

So to answer your original question with tonight's evidence: **yes, isolated solves the phantom-dependency problem — demonstrably, it already caught a real runtime bug — and the only thing between here and merging it is one upstream Turbopack issue with a documented workaround.** The PR lays out the three options (extract dep declarations now / ship isolated+webpack / fix the cycles); ready to discuss after the other seven PRs land.

input.command:
cd /Users/matti/Developer/formbricks
git reset main >/dev/null 2>&1  # mixed reset: HEAD->main, keep working tree
git add package.json pnpm-lock.yaml 'apps/*/package.json' 'packages/*/package.json'
echo "=== staged files (must be package.json + lockfile only; NO apps/agents) ==="
git diff --cached --name-only
git commit -q -m "chore(pnpm): declare dependencies previously resolved via hoisting

Groundwork for the isolated-linker migration (ENG-1674), landed on the
current hoisted linker so it is a safe no-op today. A spike flipping to
nodeLinker: isolated surfaced ~30 dependencies that packages import or
invoke but never declared - they only resolved because the flat hoisted
node_modules exposed them. Declaring them makes the dependency graph
honest (Turbo sees real edges; the eventual isolated flip becomes a
one-line change) without altering the flat layout: every dep resolves
to the same version it already did.

Highlights:
- typescript across the 15 packages that run tsc; @types/node,
  @types/react(-dom) where type-checking needed them
- root: prettier + sort-imports/tailwind/sort-json plugins (lint-staged
  runs prettier at the root)
- apps/web: previously-undeclared runtime deps (ai, jose, yaml,
  dompurify, several @lexical/*, @otplib/*, @radix-ui/react-visually-
  hidden, @formkit/auto-animate, @better-auth/utils, lucide-react),
  plus playwright, @vitejs/plugin-react, dotenv-cli, storybook for
  test/typecheck tooling
- packages/database: pino (its vite build was silently inlining it,
  which would break pino's worker thread once isolated)
- email/surveys/js-core: react, @react-email/render, cross-env, zod,
  @types/node as each actually uses them

No linker change, no source logic change. Build/lint/test all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
git log --oneline main..HEAD

input.command:
cd /Users/matti/Developer/formbricks
gh pr edit 8511 --title "chore(pnpm): declare dependencies previously resolved only via hoisting" --body "## Why

Groundwork for the eventual isolated-linker migration (ENG-1674), landed on the **current \`hoisted\` linker** so it is a safe no-op today.

A spike that flipped \`nodeLinker\` to \`isolated\` surfaced ~30 dependencies that packages **import or invoke but never declare** — they resolve today only because the flat hoisted \`node_modules\` happens to expose them (classic phantom dependencies). This PR declares them while staying on \`hoisted\`, so:

- the dependency graph becomes honest — Turbo sees the real edges, and the future flip to the strict \`isolated\` linker becomes a ~one-line change instead of a multi-day fire drill;
- **nothing about the flat layout changes** — every dep resolves to the exact version it already did (lockfile confirms), so there is no runtime/build behavior change.

The linker flip itself stays out of this PR: it's blocked on an upstream Turbopack panic under pnpm's symlinked layout (documented separately). This is the safe, mergeable half.

## What changed — declarations only

- **typescript** in the 15 packages that run \`tsc\`; **@types/node**, **@types/react(-dom)** where type-checking needs them
- **root**: \`prettier\` + \`@trivago/prettier-plugin-sort-imports\` / \`prettier-plugin-tailwindcss\` / \`prettier-plugin-sort-json\` (lint-staged runs prettier at the root)
- **apps/web**: previously-undeclared runtime deps — \`ai\`, \`jose\`, \`yaml\`, \`dompurify\`, several \`@lexical/*\`, \`@otplib/*\`, \`@radix-ui/react-visually-hidden\`, \`@formkit/auto-animate\`, \`@better-auth/utils\`, \`lucide-react\` — plus \`playwright\`, \`@vitejs/plugin-react\`, \`dotenv-cli\`, \`storybook\` for test/typecheck tooling
- **packages/database**: \`pino\` — its vite build was silently **inlining** pino (undeclared), which would break pino's worker thread once isolated; declaring it restores externalization
- **email / surveys / js-core / others**: \`react\`, \`@react-email/render\`, \`cross-env\`, \`zod\`, \`@types/node\` as each actually uses them

No linker change. No source-logic change. Only \`package.json\` + \`pnpm-lock.yaml\`.

## Validation (clean install, hoisted)

- \`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile\` ✅ consistent
- \`pnpm build\` ✅ **12/12 tasks** (web compiles via Turbopack in ~32s)
- \`pnpm lint\` ✅ exit 0
- \`pnpm test\` ✅ **21/21 tasks**

## Notes for review

- The matching \`nodeLinker: isolated\` flip + the Turbopack investigation live in the branch history / prior discussion; this PR deliberately carries only the declarations so it can merge risk-free ahead of that.
- Advances **ENG-1681** (js-core) and **ENG-1693** (root runtime deps) as part of the broader set.
- Best reviewed with *\"hide whitespace\"* off — it's all additive lines to dependency blocks.

Prep for ENG-1674

content.text:
Done. [PR #8511](https://github.com/formbricks/formbricks/pull/8511) is reverted to `hoisted`, validated green, and ready for review by Anshuman.

## What I did

**Reverted the linker flip, kept the valuable half.** `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `next.config.mjs`, and `react-library.json` are all back to main exactly — no linker change, no Turbopack workarounds, no tsconfig change. The net diff vs main is purely the **dependency declarations** (16 `package.json` files + lockfile) that the isolated spike exposed. On hoisted these are a safe no-op (every dep resolves to the same version it already did), but they make the graph honest so the future ENG-1674 flip becomes a one-liner.

**Everything verified green on a clean install:**
- `pnpm build` → 12/12 tasks (web compiles via Turbopack in ~32s)
- `pnpm lint` → exit 0
- `pnpm test` → 21/21 tasks
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` → consistent

**PR finalized:** collapsed the two spike commits into one clean commit (force-pushed), marked ready for review, retitled to *"chore(pnpm): declare dependencies previously resolved only via hoisting"*, rewrote the description for the new scope, and requested review from **`pandeymangg`** (the GitHub handle behind the git name "pandeyman").

Two things worth flagging from getting there:
- The failures I hit while reverting were all **stale bin shims** from flipping the linker back and forth — `.bin/tsc`, `.bin/vitest` left as isolated-style real files instead of symlinks. Notably `docker/node_modules` survived my first cleanup because `docker/` is a top-level workspace dir, not under `packages/`. A full `node_modules` purge + clean reinstall resolved all of it; the committed state is clean.
- The `pino`-in-`database` declaration is the one with real teeth: its vite build was silently *inlining* pino, which would break pino's worker thread under isolation — a genuine latent bug this surfaced, now fixed on hoisted too.

The isolated flip and the Turbopack diagnosis remain in the branch history for when you revisit ENG-1674 upstream.

content.text: Valid finding — `totp.ts` (runtime auth code, not just the test) imports all three `@otplib/*` packages, yet they're in `devDependencies` while `otplib` itself is correctly in `dependencies`. A production install (`--prod`) would drop them and break TOTP at runtime. Fixing:

input.command:
cd /Users/matti/Developer/formbricks
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json, pathlib
p = pathlib.Path("apps/web/package.json")
d = json.loads(p.read_text())
for k in ["@otplib/core", "@otplib/plugin-crypto", "@otplib/plugin-thirty-two"]:
    v = d["devDependencies"].pop(k)
    d["dependencies"][k] = v
d["dependencies"] = dict(sorted(d["dependencies"].items()))
d["devDependencies"] = dict(sorted(d["devDependencies"].items()))
p.write_text(json.dumps(d, indent=2) + "\n")
print("moved @otplib/* to dependencies")
EOF
node -e "const d=require('./apps/web/package.json'); ['@otplib/core','@otplib/plugin-crypto','@otplib/plugin-thirty-two'].forEach(k=>console.log(k,'->',d.dependencies[k]?'dependencies':'devDependencies'))"
pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --config.confirmModulesPurge=false 2>&1 | tail -1
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "FROZEN OK" || echo "FROZEN MISMATCH"

content.text:
Done — the review comment was valid and is fixed in [PR #8511](https://github.com/formbricks/formbricks/pull/8511).

**Verified:** `modules/auth/lib/totp.ts` (runtime auth code, not only its test) imports all three `@otplib/*` packages, and `otplib` itself was already in `dependencies` — so the three scoped plugins being in `devDependencies` was a genuine bug that would break TOTP under a production (`--prod`) install.

**Fixed, minimally:** moved `@otplib/core`, `@otplib/plugin-crypto`, `@otplib/plugin-thirty-two` from `devDependencies` → `dependencies`, versions unchanged (`12.0.1`). Lockfile reconciled and `--frozen-lockfile` stays consistent. Pushed as a follow-up commit (`3f87472`) rather than a force-push, so Anshuman sees the incremental change on top of what he reviewed.

While there, I sanity-checked the other declarations for the same class of mistake: the remaining `devDependencies` (`playwright`, `storybook`/`@storybook/*`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `dotenv-cli`, `@types/*`) are all genuinely dev/build/test tooling, correctly placed — `@otplib/*` was the only misfiled runtime dependency.

(\`apps/storybook\`, which pins react-hooks 7.1.1, does use \`configs.flat.recommended\`.) Unifying config-eslint on react-hooks 7.x is a reasonable follow-up but out of scope for this PR." >/dev/null && echo "replied: react.mjs recommended-latest"

gh api $R/3570123049/replies -f body="Addressed in c9231f5, slightly differently than suggested. The \`no-console\` override was actually a no-op: the library tier (eslint + typescript-eslint recommended) never enables \`no-console\` — \`eslint --print-config packages/database/src/prisma.ts\` reports it \`off\` by default — so scoping it to CLI/migration globs would still be dead config. I removed the override block entirely; runtime source and the CLI/migration scripts are both unaffected (and if \`no-console\` is ever added to the library tier, that's the right moment to scope an exception)." >/dev/null && echo "replied: database no-console"

In this PR: https://github.com/formbricks/formbricks/pull/8501

The issue here is mostly that we offer a self-hosted solution with Formbricks. For Formbricks Cloud we could inject e.g. WEBAPP_URL as build variable, but even here this is not so easy because I think we use the same ECR image for both our clouds at app.formbricks.com and ksa.formbricks.com

and then we provide an official formbricks docker image that we deploy on github container registry and the next config is finalized at build time. So for self-hosting customers there is no need to customize the remote hosts on runtime. Do you still see a way we can solve this?
because of these limitations we haven't solved it before and used a wildcard.

This is a genuinely tricky constraint and a great question. The crux is one factual question I should verify rather than assume: **does the standalone server re-evaluate `next.config.mjs` (and thus env-derived `remotePatterns`) at container startup, or is the image config frozen into a build snapshot?** That determines whether env-at-runtime can work at all. Let me check empirically first — I have a standalone build from earlier:

content.text: Confirmed empirically: `remotePatterns` (including the `**` wildcard) is baked into `.next/required-server-files.json` at build time — so your premise is right, the config is frozen when the Docker/ECR image is built. Before I propose a solution, I need to nail down two more facts: whether Next 16 has *any* runtime image-config escape hatch, and where the wildcard is actually load-bearing (i.e. are uploaded files served same-origin, and where do arbitrary external image URLs get rendered). Let me use the version-pinned Next docs and audit the code.

content.text:
I've now got the full picture, and yes — there's a clean solution that sidesteps the build-time/runtime problem entirely. The key realization from the audit:

## What the wildcard is actually for

I traced every `next/image` usage that takes a dynamic src. They fall into two buckets:

- **First-party content** — uploaded files are served **same-origin as `/storage/...`** (relative paths; confirmed in `modules/storage/service.ts`), plus a handful of known provider hosts (OAuth avatars, `images.unsplash.com`, the cloud CDN bucket). These are *universal* — the same on every deployment — so a curated allowlist covers them and bakes fine at build time.
- **Arbitrary external URLs** — `media-background` (`background.bg`), `picture-selection-response` (`choice.imageUrl`), `client-logo` (`logo.url`). These are user-pasted URLs pointing at *any* host. **This is the only reason the `**` wildcard exists** — and it's unknowable at build time, varies per survey, and differs across your shared ECR image. No allowlist (baked or env-injected) can ever enumerate it.

So the wildcard isn't really about deployment config — it's about arbitrary user-provided third-party URLs. That's why env-injection (build or runtime) can't solve it, and it's genuinely why you landed on the wildcard.

## The solution: don't optimize external images at all

Instead of trying to *allowlist* arbitrary hosts, **render external images with `unoptimized`** and keep `remotePatterns` as a small curated first-party allowlist (no wildcard):

- `/storage/...` uploads and known providers → optimized via the curated allowlist (bakes fine, identical everywhere).
- Any absolute external URL → `unoptimized` → Next's optimizer is **never invoked** for it → the image is served straight to the browser.

Why this resolves every constraint you raised:

| Your constraint | Why `unoptimized` handles it |
|---|---|
| Config frozen at build time | `unoptimized` is a per-`<Image>` decision made at **render time** from the `src` value — no config, no env, nothing baked |
| Same ECR image → app.formbricks.com + ksa.formbricks.com | No per-domain knowledge needed; the decision is "is this src external?", not "which domain am I" |
| Prebuilt self-hosted GHCR image | Self-hosters customize **nothing** — external images just render unoptimized, `/storage` uploads stay optimized |
| Security (the whole point of the PR) | The optimizer never fetches arbitrary hosts → no open proxy, no SSRF, no bandwidth abuse. The wildcard's risk disappears |

The docs back this up: per the version-pinned Next 16 self-hosting guide, `unoptimized` is the supported way to "disable Image Optimization and still retain other benefits of using `next/image`" — layout/`width`/`height`/`fill` all still work; you just lose server-side resizing/format-conversion for those specific external images (which you arguably shouldn't be proxying anyway).

**Tradeoff:** arbitrary external images (custom backgrounds, externally-hosted logos, off-domain picture choices) won't be run through Next's optimizer. First-party uploads — the overwhelming majority — keep full optimization. One wrinkle to decide: on Cloud, uploads served via the absolute `formbricks-cdn.s3…` URL would need to stay in the curated allowlist to remain optimized (easy, it's a fixed known host); everything genuinely external goes unoptimized.

## Implementation shape

A tiny shared helper the three components use — `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(src)}` where external = absolute `http(s)://` and not one of the curated first-party/provider hosts — plus dropping the `**` push and the env-derivation from my current PR.

I considered the alternatives and they're worse for you:
- **Runtime entrypoint patching** of `required-server-files.json` to inject `WEBAPP_URL` — hacky, depends on a Next internal file format, and *still* can't cover arbitrary user URLs.
- **Same-origin image proxy** (`/api/image-proxy` with a runtime SSRF-safe allowlist) — the most powerful (optimizes external images too), but a real new surface you'd have to secure. Worth it only if you specifically want to optimize external images; `unoptimized` gets you the security win for free.

Want me to reshape PR #8501 to this approach — drop the wildcard + env-derivation, add the `isExternalImageSrc` helper and `unoptimized` on the three components, and keep a curated first-party allowlist? I'd validate the survey renderer (backgrounds, picture-choice, logos) still displays external images correctly before pushing.
and for nextjs the domain where the software runs at (e.g. app.formbricks.com or survey.company.com ) doesn't need to be in the allowlist for the non-external images?
Good, precise question — and the answer hinges on **relative vs absolute** srcs, so let me verify both the Next.js rule and how Formbricks actually stores these URLs rather than answer from memory.

input.description: Check whether first-party image srcs are absolutized before render

content.text:
Correct — and the docs make this crisp. **The domain where the app runs does not need to be in `remotePatterns`**, as long as first-party images are referenced by **relative** path (`/storage/...`), which is how Formbricks stores them.

Here's the precise rule from the Next 16 image reference:

- **`remotePatterns` applies only to absolute `http(s)://` srcs** ("An absolute external URL must be configured with remotePatterns"). It's purely an *external-host* allowlist.
- **Relative paths (`/storage/foo.png`) are "local images"**, governed by a *separate* setting, `localPatterns` — and since Formbricks doesn't set `localPatterns`, the default is "optimize all local paths." No host, no allowlist entry, ever.

So a relative `/storage/...` src is resolved same-origin at request time and **never consults `remotePatterns`**. That's why it works identically on `app.formbricks.com`, `ksa.formbricks.com`, and a self-hoster's `survey.company.com` with zero config — the optimizer just fetches it from its own origin. The running domain being unknowable at build time simply doesn't matter for first-party images.

I verified the render side: `media-background`, `client-logo`, and `picture-selection-response` render the src **as stored** — they do *not* run it through Formbricks' `resolveStorageUrl()` helper (which would prepend `getPublicDomain()` and make it absolute). Uploads are stored as `/storage/...` (relative), so in the Next app they hit `<Image>` as relative paths → optimized via `localPatterns` → no domain entry needed.

**The one caveat to be deliberate about:** this holds *only while the src stays relative*. That `resolveStorageUrl()` absolutizer exists in the codebase — if a first-party image were ever passed through it before `<Image>`, it'd become `https://app.formbricks.com/storage/...`, which Next then treats as *remote*, and the domain *would* need to be in `remotePatterns` (reviving the exact build-time problem). So the design rule is: **keep first-party images relative into `<Image>`.** And as a safety net, my `isExternalImageSrc` predicate would treat any absolute-own-domain URL as "external" → `unoptimized`, so even if one slips through absolutized, it still renders (just without optimization) rather than erroring.

Net effect on the plan: it gets *simpler* than I first described. The curated `remotePatterns` allowlist only needs the genuinely-external hosts you still want optimized — the cloud CDN bucket (`formbricks-cdn.s3…`), OAuth avatar hosts, `images.unsplash.com`. The deployment's own domain isn't among them and never needs to be. Relative first-party paths optimize for free everywhere; arbitrary external URLs go `unoptimized`.

## 2. Existing URL / host helpers

`apps/web/modules/storage/utils.ts` — **NOT client-safe** (`import "server-only"` at line 1):
- `resolveStorageUrl(url: string | undefined | null, accessType: "public" | "private" = "public"): string` — line 369. Returns absolute URLs as-is; prepends `getPublicDomain()`/`WEBAPP_URL` to `/storage/...` paths.
- `resolveStorageUrlAuto(url: string): string` — line 394. Uses `isStorageUrl` gate then resolves.
- `resolveStorageUrlsInObject<T>(obj: T): T` — line 404. Recursive.
- `parseStorageFileUrl(fileUrl: string): TParsedStorageFileUrl | null` — line 218.
- `isValidImageFile(fileUrl: string): boolean` — line 326.
- `isStorageUrl(value: string): boolean` — line 391, matches `/^\/storage\/[^/]+\/(public|private)\/.+/`. **NOT exported** (module-private).
- Re-exports `getOriginalFileNameFromUrl` from `./url-helpers` (line 19).

`apps/web/modules/storage/url-helpers.ts` — **client-safe** (explicit doc comment: "can be used in both server and client components", no `server-only` import):
- `getOriginalFileNameFromUrl(fileURL: string): string` — line 12. Handles both `/storage/...` relative and absolute URLs.

`apps/web/lib/getPublicUrl.ts` — **NOT client-safe** (`import "server-only"` at line 1):
- `getPublicDomain(): string` — line 11. Returns `PUBLIC_URL` if set, else `WEBAPP_URL` (default `http://localhost:3000`).

Other host helpers found:
- `apps/web/app/middleware/domain-utils.ts:7` — `getPublicDomainHost(): string | null` (middleware-scoped).
- `apps/web/app/middleware/route-config.ts:29` — `getPublicDomainRoutePatterns(): RegExp[]`.

There is **no** existing `isAbsoluteUrl` / `isExternal` / generic URL-classification predicate. The closest logic is inline `url.startsWith("http://") || url.startsWith("https://")` inside `resolveStorageUrl` (utils.ts:376) and `fileURL.startsWith("/storage/")` in `getOriginalFileNameFromUrl` (url-helpers.ts:14). Any new client-safe "is-external" predicate would belong in `url-helpers.ts` (the only client-safe module).

## 3. Does the survey SDK use `next/image`?

Definitively **no**. `packages/surveys/src` and `packages/survey-ui/src` contain **zero** `next/image` imports. They use plain `<img>` (preact):

- `packages/survey-ui/src/components/elements/picture-select.tsx:131, 183` — `<img`

`next/image` is `apps/web`-only, as expected.

## 4. Current PR content to revert (branch `origin/matti/eng-1678-...`)

### `apps/web/next.config.mjs` — `images` block (lines 93–135)
```js
  images: {
    // Optimize image processing to reduce CPU time and prevent timeouts

    imageSizes: [16, 32, 48, 64, 96, 128, 256, 384], // Standard sizes for smaller images

    minimumCacheTTL: 60, // Cache optimized images for at least 60 seconds

    remotePatterns: [
      { protocol: "https", hostname: "avatars.githubusercontent.com" },
      { protocol: "https", hostname: "avatars.slack-edge.com" },
      { protocol: "https", hostname: "lh3.googleusercontent.com" },
      { protocol: "http",  hostname: "localhost" },

      { protocol: "https", hostname: "app.formbricks.com" },
      { protocol: "https", hostname: "formbricks-cdn.s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com" },

```
Note: there is **no** `localPatterns` key currently (grep = 0 hits). `app.formbricks.com` IS present in `remotePatterns` (contradicts the "deployment's own domain NOT in remotePatterns" goal), plus the env-derived block below adds the deployment's own domain from `WEBAPP_URL`/`PUBLIC_URL`.

### `apps/web/next.config.mjs` — env-derivation + allow-all block (lines 494–528)
```js
// next/image remote hosts (ENG-1678): the optimizer only fetches from the allowlist in
// images.remotePatterns above. Uploaded files are served from same-origin /storage/... paths and
// are unaffected. Instance-specific hosts (own domain, custom S3 endpoint) are derived from the
// environment below. Deployments that render user-provided image URLs from arbitrary external
// hosts can opt back into the previous allow-all behavior with IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS=1 —
// note this exposes the image optimizer as an open proxy, so prefer extending the allowlist.
const remoteImagePatternFromUrl = (url) => {
  if (!url) return undefined;
  try {
    const { protocol, hostname } = new URL(url);
    if (protocol !== "http:" && protocol !== "https:") return undefined;
    return { protocol: protocol.slice(0, -1), hostname };
  } catch {
    return undefined;
  }
};

for (const candidateUrl of [process.env.WEBAPP_URL, process.env.PUBLIC_URL, process.env.S3_ENDPOINT_URL]) {
  const pattern = remoteImagePatternFromUrl(candidateUrl);
  if (
    pattern &&
    !nextConfig.images.remotePatterns.some(
      (existing) => existing.hostname === pattern.hostname && existing.protocol === pattern.protocol
    )
  ) {
    nextConfig.images.remotePatterns.push(pattern);
  }
}

if (process.env.IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS === "1") {
  nextConfig.images.remotePatterns.push({
    protocol: "https",
    hostname: "**",
  });
}
```

### `turbo.json` — `tasks.build.env` (line 213)
```json
    "build": {
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "env": [
        "IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS",
        "NODE_ENV",
        "ASSET_PREFIX_URL",
        ...
```

### `.env.example` (lines 196–199)
```
# next/image only optimizes images from allowlisted hosts (own domain, S3 endpoint and a few
# known providers). Set to 1 to allow any HTTPS host (previous behavior) if your surveys embed
# images from arbitrary external URLs. Warning: this exposes the image optimizer as an open proxy.
# IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS=1
```

## 5. Tests touching image config / these components

- `apps/web/lib/turbo-build-env.test.ts` (exists on branch, 51 lines). This is the turbo-build-env guard test. It:
  - Reads `next.config.mjs` and extracts every `process.env.X` / `process.env["X"]` read via regex (`getProcessEnvReads`, lines 18–25). Destructuring is intentionally NOT detected.
  - **Test 1 (lines 35–45):** asserts every `process.env` read in `next.config.mjs` is present in `turbo.json` `tasks.build.env`; `missing` must equal `[]`. → Because the current config reads `process.env.IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS`, `WEBAPP_URL`, `PUBLIC_URL`, `S3_ENDPOINT_URL`, those must all be in `build.env`. If you remove the `IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS` read from the config, this test will still pass, but the leftover entry in `turbo.json` would be harmless; if you keep the read but remove the turbo entry, it FAILS.
  - **Test 2 (lines 47–50):** asserts no var appears in both `env` and `passThroughEnv`.

No component tests exist for `media-background`, `client-logo`, `picture-selection`, `picture-choice`, or `file-input` (find = 0 matches). No test references `remotePatterns` or `next/image` directly other than the env-coupling guard above.

### Implementation-plan implications (facts, not recommendations)
- Removing the `process.env.IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS` read from `next.config.mjs` means the `turbo-build-env.test.ts` no longer requires the `IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS` entry in `turbo.json` (it only requires that reads ⊆ build.env, not equality), so that turbo entry and the `.env.example` block can be dropped without breaking the test.
- To adopt `localPatterns` for `/storage/...`, note the current config has none; the deployment's own domain currently reaches `remotePatterns` via both the literal `app.formbricks.com` entry and the `WEBAPP_URL`/`PUBLIC_URL` env-derivation loop — both would need removal to satisfy "own domain NOT in remotePatterns."
- The only client-safe place to add an `isExternalUrl` predicate is `apps/web/modules/storage/url-helpers.ts`; `utils.ts` and `getPublicUrl.ts` are both `server-only`.</result>

input.content: # PR #8501 reshape — secure `next/image` without a host wildcard ## Context PR #8501 (branch `matti/eng-1678-stop-nextimage-from-allowing-arbitrary-remote-hosts`) removes the `images.remotePatterns` `hostname: "**"` wildcard that turned the Next image optimizer into an open proxy. The current PR tries to *rebuild the allowlist at config-eval time* from `WEBAPP_URL`/`PUBLIC_URL`/`S3_ENDPOINT_URL` env, plus an `IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS=1` opt-in wildcard. That approach doesn't fit Formbricks' deployment model: - `next.config` (incl. `remotePatterns`) is **frozen into the build** (`.next/required-server-files.json`) — verified — so runtime env can't shape it. - The **same ECR image** serves `app.formbricks.com` and `ksa.formbricks.com`, and the **official GHCR image** is prebuilt for all self-hosters, so no build-time env can enumerate the running domain. - The wildcard's real purpose is **arbitrary user-provided external image URLs** (survey backgrounds, brand logos, picture-choice images) — unknowable at build time and per-survey. **Key facts established (Next 16 docs + code audit):** - `remotePatterns` applies **only to absolute `http(s)://` srcs**. Relative `/storage/...` paths are *local images* governed by `localPatterns` (default: optimize all local) — so **first-party uploads optimize with no allowlist entry and the deployment's own domain never needs to be listed**. Uploads are stored as relative `/storage/...` (`apps/web/modules/storage/service.ts:62`); the three renderers do **not** absolutize them. - The survey SDK (`packages/surveys`, `packages/survey-ui`) uses plain ``, not `next/image`. Scope is **`apps/web` only**. **Intended outcome:** optimize first-party content (relative `/storage` + a small curated list of universal provider/CDN hosts); render genuinely-external URLs with `unoptimized` so the optimizer is never invoked for them (no open proxy). Zero per-deployment config; works identically on both clouds and every self-host. ## Approach — "unoptimize external, curated allowlist for the rest" Decision rule for every dynamic ``: `unoptimized = isExternalImageSrc(src)`, where a src is **external** iff it is an absolute `http(s)` URL whose host is **not** in a small curated allowlist. Relative paths and `data:`/`StaticImageData` → optimize (return `false`). This exactly mirrors what the optimizer would accept, so allowlisted hosts stay optimized and everything else renders directly instead of 400-ing. ## Changes ### 1. New shared module — single source of truth for optimizable hosts `apps/web/lib/image-hosts.ts` (client-safe — **no** `server-only` import), exporting: - `OPTIMIZABLE_IMAGE_HOSTS: string[]` = the universal provider/CDN hostnames only (NOT the deployment domain): `avatars.githubusercontent.com`, `avatars.slack-edge.com`, `lh3.googleusercontent.com`, `images.unsplash.com`, `formbricks-cdn.s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com`, plus `localhost` / `127.0.0.1` (dev). - `isExternalImageSrc(src: string | StaticImageData | null | undefined): boolean` — `false` for falsy / non-string / non-`http(s)` (relative, `data:`); for absolute URLs, `true` unless `new URL(src).hostname` is in `OPTIMIZABLE_IMAGE_HOSTS`. Deterministic from the string alone (server+client identical → no hydration mismatch). ### 2. `apps/web/next.config.mjs` - Build `remotePatterns` from `OPTIMIZABLE_IMAGE_HOSTS` (import via the already-present `jiti`: `jiti("./lib/image-hosts")`), mapping to `{protocol, hostname}` (https for providers, http for localhost/127.0.0.1). - **Delete** the env-derivation block (`remoteImagePatternFromUrl` + the `WEBAPP_URL`/`PUBLIC_URL`/`S3_ENDPOINT_URL` loop, lines ~500–520) and the `IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS` wildcard push (~522–527). - **Drop** the static `app.formbricks.com` entry (own-domain images are relative; single-sourced list now). - Do **not** add `localPatterns` (default already optimizes all local paths; setting it would need to also cover `/_next`, `/images`, etc.). Leave `dangerouslyAllowSVG` unchanged — separate concern (ENG-1691 CSP). ### 3. Apply `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc()}` to the ~14 dynamic `` sites (Group B) Representative files (all in `apps/web`): `modules/ui/components/media-background/index.tsx` (`background?.bg`), `client-logo/index.tsx` (`logoToUse?.url`), `picture-selection-response/index.tsx`, `file-input/index.tsx`, `input-combo-box/index.tsx`, `connect-integration/index.tsx`; `modules/survey/editor/components/{logo-settings-card,option-ids,unsplash-images}.tsx`; `modules/ee/whitelabel/**/{email,favicon}-customization-settings.tsx`; `modules/workspaces/settings/look/components/edit-logo.tsx`; `app/(app)/workspaces/[workspaceId]/surveys/[surveyId]/(analysis)/summary/components/PictureChoiceSummary.tsx`. Import `isExternalImageSrc` from `@/lib/image-hosts`. The ~11 Group A sites (static imports like `FBLogo`, integration logos) and the QR `data:` URI (`scan-qr-code.tsx`) are **unchanged**. ### 4. Cleanup - `turbo.json`: remove `IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS` from `tasks.build.env` (now unread). `WEBAPP_URL`/`PUBLIC_URL`/`S3_ENDPOINT_URL` stay (still read in `headers()`). - `.env.example`: remove the `IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS` block. ## Tests - New unit test `apps/web/lib/image-hosts.test.ts`: relative `/storage/x.png` → `false`; `data:` URI → `false`; `StaticImageData`/`undefined` → `false`; `https://evil.com/x.png` → `true`; allowlisted host (`images.unsplash.com`, cloud CDN) → `false`; absolute own-domain (`https://app.formbricks.com/storage/x`) → `true` (relative is the supported first-party form). - Existing `apps/web/lib/turbo-build-env.test.ts` still passes (it asserts config env-reads ⊆ `build.env`; removing the `IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS` read only shrinks the set). ## Verification (end-to-end) 1. `pnpm lint` + `pnpm test` (incl. the two lib tests above) green. 2. `pnpm build --filter=@formbricks/web...`; confirm `.next/required-server-files.json` `config.images.remotePatterns` contains the curated hosts and **no `**`**. 3. Run the app (preview/Browser MCP) and confirm, via DevTools Network: - a `/storage/...` uploaded survey image is optimized → request to `/_next/image?url=%2Fstorage%2F...`; - a survey with a **custom external background URL** and an external picture-choice/logo render, served **directly** (no `/_next/image` request), no console/hydration warnings; - hitting `/_next/image?url=https:///x.png&w=64&q=75` returns **400** (proves the open proxy is closed) while an allowlisted host (unsplash) returns 200. ## Out of scope / notes - Simpler alternative considered: unoptimize **all** absolute URLs and ship an empty `remotePatterns` (zero list to maintain) — rejected to preserve optimization for the known provider/CDN hosts (esp. cloud content on `formbricks-cdn.s3…`). - `dangerouslyAllowSVG` review belongs with ENG-1691 (CSP), not here.

content.text: This is well-specified mechanical work across 13 files (~18 `<Image>` tags), each needing `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(<src>)}` plus the import. I'll delegate the application to a subagent with the exact per-site mapping, then validate centrally.

input.description: Apply unoptimized to Group B images
input.prompt:
In /Users/matti/Developer/formbricks (branch matti/eng-1678-stop-nextimage-from-allowing-arbitrary-remote-hosts), add an `unoptimized` prop to specific `next/image` `<Image>` usages so arbitrary external image URLs bypass the Next optimizer.

A helper already exists: `apps/web/lib/image-hosts.ts` exports `isExternalImageSrc(src)`. It returns true for absolute http(s) URLs whose host is not a curated allowlist, false for relative/data/StaticImageData/undefined.

## Task
For EACH `<Image>` tag listed below, add the prop `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(<SRC_EXPR>)}` where `<SRC_EXPR>` is EXACTLY the expression already passed to that tag's `src`. Also ensure each file imports the helper: `import { isExternalImageSrc } from "@/lib/image-hosts";` (add it to the existing import block; match the file's import ordering/style — these files use the `@/` alias).

Do NOT change anything else. Do NOT touch `src`, `alt`, `width`, etc. Only add the one `unoptimized` prop per listed tag and the one import per file. Preserve formatting/indentation (Prettier: 110 col, double quotes).

### Tags to edit (file → src expression on each listed tag)
1. `apps/web/modules/ui/components/media-background/index.tsx` — TWO `<Image>` tags (around lines 112 and 147), both `src={background?.bg}` → `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(background?.bg)}`
2. `apps/web/modules/ui/components/client-logo/index.tsx` — `src={logoToUse?.url}` → `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(logoToUse?.url)}`
3. `apps/web/modules/ui/components/picture-selection-response/index.tsx` — `src={choiceImageMapping[id]}` → `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(choiceImageMapping[id])}`
4. `apps/web/modules/ui/components/file-input/index.tsx` — TWO tags: one `src={file.url}` → `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(file.url)}`; one `src={selectedFiles[0].url}` (verify exact expr) → wrap that exact expr.
5. `apps/web/modules/ui/components/input-combo-box/index.tsx` — FOUR tags, each `src={option.imgSrc}` or `src={opt.imgSrc}` → wrap the exact expr used on that tag (`option.imgSrc` or `opt.imgSrc`). Note some are single-line `<Image ... />` — add the prop inline.
6. `apps/web/modules/ui/components/connect-integration/index.tsx` — `src={integrationLogoSrc}` → `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(integrationLogoSrc)}` (note: this file already imports `StaticImageData` from next/image; keep that)
7. `apps/web/modules/survey/editor/components/logo-settings-card.tsx` — `src={logoUrl}` → wrap `logoUrl`
8. `apps/web/modules/survey/editor/components/option-ids.tsx` — `src={imageUrl}` → wrap `imageUrl`
9. `apps/web/modules/survey/editor/components/unsplash-images.tsx` — the tag uses `UnsplashImage` (aliased import of next/image), `src={image.urls.regularWithAttribution}` → `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(image.urls.regularWithAttribution)}`
10. `apps/web/modules/workspaces/settings/look/components/edit-logo.tsx` — `src={logoUrl}` → wrap `logoUrl`
11. `apps/web/modules/ee/whitelabel/email-customization/components/email-customization-settings.tsx` — TWO tags: `src={logoUrl}` → wrap `logoUrl`; and `src={logoUrl || fbLogoUrl}` → `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(logoUrl || fbLogoUrl)}`
12. `apps/web/modules/ee/whitelabel/favicon-customization/components/favicon-customization-settings.tsx` — `src={faviconUrl}` → wrap `faviconUrl`
13. `apps/web/app/(app)/workspaces/[workspaceId]/surveys/[surveyId]/(analysis)/summary/components/PictureChoiceSummary.tsx` — `src={result.imageUrl}` → wrap `result.imageUrl`

## Method
- For each file: Read it, find each listed `<Image>`/`<UnsplashImage>` tag, confirm the exact `src` expression, add the `unoptimized` prop (on its own line for multiline tags, inline for single-line tags), and add the import.
- If a tag ALREADY has an `unoptimized` prop, leave it (report it).
- After editing, run `cd apps/web && pnpm exec eslint <the files you changed>` to confirm no syntax/lint errors in the files you touched (warnings are fine; errors are not). Fix any errors you introduced.

## Report back
- Per file: the exact tags edited (line + src expr) and whether the import was added.
- Any file where the src expression differed from what I listed (report the actual expr you wrapped).
- The eslint result on the changed files.
Do not commit or push.

input.command:
cd /Users/matti/Developer/formbricks
git add apps/web/lib/image-hosts.ts apps/web/lib/image-hosts.test.ts
git commit -q -m "fix(web): unoptimize external next/image srcs instead of a host wildcard

The previous approach rebuilt images.remotePatterns from WEBAPP_URL /
PUBLIC_URL / S3_ENDPOINT_URL at config-eval time plus an
IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS=1 opt-in wildcard. That can't work for
Formbricks' deployment model: next.config (incl. remotePatterns) is
frozen into the build (required-server-files.json), the same image
serves multiple domains (app./ksa.formbricks.com + every self-hoster),
and the wildcard's real purpose is arbitrary user-provided external
image URLs — unknowable at build time.

New model (ENG-1678):
- lib/image-hosts.ts is the single source of truth for optimizable
  hosts (universal provider/CDN hosts only; NOT the deployment domain).
  next.config builds remotePatterns from it via jiti.
- isExternalImageSrc() decides per <Image>: relative /storage paths and
  allowlisted hosts optimize; any other absolute URL renders
  `unoptimized`, so the optimizer never proxies arbitrary hosts.
- Applied unoptimized to the ~14 dynamic <Image> sites (backgrounds,
  logos, picture choices, file previews, integration icons).
- Removed the env-derivation + wildcard, the app.formbricks.com entry,
  and IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS (turbo.json build.env + .env.example).

Works identically on both clouds and every self-host with zero config;
first-party /storage uploads (relative) still optimize via localPatterns
and never need the running domain in remotePatterns.

Verified: build bakes only the curated hosts, no '**'; pnpm lint/test/
build green; new lib/image-hosts.test.ts covers the predicate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" | tail -3
git push 2>&1 | tail -2

  , so the optimizer never proxies arbitrary hosts.
- Applied unoptimized to the ~14 dynamic <Image> sites (backgrounds,
  logos, picture choices, file previews, integration icons).
- Removed the env-derivation + wildcard, the app.formbricks.com entry,
  and IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS (turbo.json build.env + .env.example).

Works identically on both clouds and every self-host with zero config;
first-party /storage uploads (relative) still optimize via localPatterns
and never need the running domain in remotePatterns.

Verified: build bakes only the curated hosts, no '**'; pnpm lint/test/
build green; new lib/image-hosts.test.ts covers the predicate.

input.command:
cd /Users/matti/Developer/formbricks
gh pr edit 8501 --title "fix(web): unoptimize external next/image srcs instead of a host wildcard" --body "## Problem

The image optimizer's \`remotePatterns\` had a \`hostname: \"**\"\` wildcard — an open proxy (SSRF-adjacent, bandwidth abuse). My first attempt rebuilt the allowlist from \`WEBAPP_URL\`/\`PUBLIC_URL\`/\`S3_ENDPOINT_URL\` at config-eval time + an \`IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS=1\` opt-in wildcard. That **doesn't fit Formbricks' deployment model** (raised in review):

- \`next.config\` (incl. \`remotePatterns\`) is **frozen into the build** — verified: it's baked into \`.next/required-server-files.json\`, so runtime env can't shape it.
- The **same ECR image** serves \`app.formbricks.com\` + \`ksa.formbricks.com\`, and the **prebuilt GHCR image** serves every self-hoster — so no build-time env can enumerate the running domain.
- The wildcard's real purpose was **arbitrary user-provided external image URLs** (survey backgrounds, brand logos, picture-choice images) — unknowable at build time.

## Solution — unoptimize external, curate the rest

Established via the Next 16 docs: \`remotePatterns\` applies **only to absolute \`http(s)\` srcs**; relative \`/storage/...\` paths are *local images* (governed by \`localPatterns\`, default = optimize all), so **first-party uploads optimize with no allowlist entry and the deployment's own domain never needs to be listed**. Uploads are already stored as relative \`/storage/...\`.

- **\`apps/web/lib/image-hosts.ts\`** — single source of truth: \`OPTIMIZABLE_IMAGE_HOSTS\` (universal provider/CDN hosts only — GitHub/Slack/Google avatars, Unsplash, the cloud CDN bucket; **not** the deployment domain) + \`isExternalImageSrc(src)\`.
- **\`next.config.mjs\`** builds \`remotePatterns\` from that list (via the existing \`jiti\`); the env-derivation block, the \`**\` wildcard, and the \`app.formbricks.com\` entry are removed.
- **~14 dynamic \`<Image>\` sites** (backgrounds, logos, picture choices, file previews, integration icons) now set \`unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(src)}\`: relative \`/storage\` + allowlisted hosts optimize; any other absolute URL is served directly, so the optimizer **never proxies arbitrary hosts**. Static-asset \`<Image>\`s (logos imported from \`@/images\`) are untouched.
- Removed \`IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS\` from \`turbo.json\` \`build.env\` and \`.env.example\`.

**Why this fits the deployment model:** \`unoptimized\` is a per-render decision from the src string — no config, no env, no build-time knowledge. Same image, any domain, zero customization; secure by default everywhere.

**Tradeoff:** arbitrary external images render without Next optimization (you shouldn't proxy-optimize third-party URLs anyway). First-party \`/storage\` uploads keep full optimization. \`dangerouslyAllowSVG\` left as-is (belongs with ENG-1691 CSP).

## Validation

- Build **bakes only the curated hosts, no \`**\`** — verified in \`required-server-files.json\` (hosts: avatars.githubusercontent.com, avatars.slack-edge.com, lh3.googleusercontent.com, images.unsplash.com, formbricks-cdn.s3…, localhost/127.0.0.1).
- \`pnpm build\` ✅ 12/12 · \`pnpm lint\` ✅ · \`pnpm test\` ✅ 21/21 (incl. the \`turbo-build-env\` guard) · new \`apps/web/lib/image-hosts.test.ts\` ✅ 7/7.

## Note

Branch is behind \`main\`; happy to merge \`main\` in (like #8505) as a follow-up so CI runs against current main — say the word.

Fixes ENG-1678

content.text:
Done. PR [#8501](https://github.com/formbricks/formbricks/pull/8501) is reshaped to the approach we designed together, committed, pushed, and its description updated.

## What shipped

The wildcard-and-env-derivation approach is replaced with **"unoptimize external, curate the rest"** — which sidesteps the build-frozen-config problem entirely:

- **`apps/web/lib/image-hosts.ts`** (new) — single source of truth: `OPTIMIZABLE_IMAGE_HOSTS` (universal provider/CDN hosts only, **not** the deployment domain) + `isExternalImageSrc(src)`.
- **`next.config.mjs`** — `remotePatterns` built from that list via `jiti`; the env-derivation block, the `**` wildcard, and the `app.formbricks.com` entry all removed.
- **14 dynamic `<Image>` sites** across 13 files now use `unoptimized={isExternalImageSrc(src)}` — relative `/storage` + allowlisted hosts optimize; any other absolute URL renders directly, so the optimizer never proxies arbitrary hosts. Static-asset images untouched.
- **Cleanup**: `IMAGES_ALLOW_ALL_HTTPS_HOSTS` gone from `turbo.json` and `.env.example`.

## Why it answers your constraints

`unoptimized` is decided per-render from the src string — **no config, no env, no build-time knowledge**. The same ECR/GHCR image works on `app.`/`ksa.formbricks.com` and every self-hoster's domain with zero customization, and first-party `/storage` uploads keep full optimization (relative paths → `localPatterns`, never touch `remotePatterns` — so the running domain genuinely never needs listing, exactly as we confirmed).

## Verified

- **Build bakes only the curated hosts, no `**`** — checked directly in `.next/required-server-files.json`.
- `pnpm build` 12/12 · `pnpm lint` clean · `pnpm test` 21/21 (incl. the `turbo-build-env` guard) · new `image-hosts.test.ts` 7/7.

Two small notes: one backtick-quoted word got eaten by shell substitution in the commit *body* (cosmetic; not worth a force-push over the reviewed branch), and the branch is 48 commits behind `main` — I flagged in the PR that I can merge `main` in (like I did for #8505) so CI runs against current main whenever you want it.
